description: Treb-Native Wormhole NTT Deployment & Lifecycle Management - Replace JSON-config + CLI approach with fully treb-native deployment and management scripts using the mento-stabletoken-ntt submodule

userStories.title: Add Wormhole infrastructure addresses to addressbook
userStories.description: As a deployer, I need Wormhole infrastructure addresses (core bridge) in the addressbook so deployment scripts can resolve them via lookupAddressbook() instead of hardcoding.
acceptanceCriteria: .treb/addressbook.json updated with WormholeCoreBridge entry for 'celo' network
.treb/addressbook.json updated with WormholeCoreBridge entry for 'monad' network
Addresses verified against official Wormhole documentation (Celo core bridge: 0xa321448d90d4e5b0A732867c18eA198e75CAC48E, Monad: check Wormhole docs)
No existing entries modified or removed
JSON is valid and properly formatted

userStories.title: Update Foundry configuration for mento-stabletoken-ntt submodule
userStories.description: As a developer, I need foundry.toml updated so contracts from the mento-stabletoken-ntt submodule compile and import correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: foundry.toml updated with remappings for mento-stabletoken-ntt (e.g. mento-stabletoken-ntt/=lib/mento-stabletoken-ntt/evm/)
Import paths like 'mento-stabletoken-ntt/src/NttManager/NttManager.sol' resolve correctly
No conflicts between submodule dependencies (forge-std, openzeppelin) and existing project dependencies
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: The submodule lives at lib/mento-stabletoken-ntt with EVM contracts under evm/src/. The submodule has its own lib/ with forge-std and openzeppelin. Check for version conflicts with the root project's forge-std.

userStories.title: Create NTTConfig Solidity library with chain and token topology structs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Solidity library defining typed structs for NTT bridge configuration so that all config is type-checked and reviewable in PRs.
acceptanceCriteria: script/config/wormhole/NTTConfig.sol exists
Defines NTTChainConfig struct with: chainName (string), evmChainId (uint256), wormholeChainId (uint16), tokenLabel (string for addressbook lookup), isBurning (bool), outboundLimit (uint256 in wei)
Defines NTTInboundLimit struct with: fromChainName (string), limit (uint256 in wei)

Defines NTTTokenConfig struct with: tokenName (string), tokenDecimals (uint8), ownerLabel (string for addressbook lookup), chains (array of NTTChainConfig), inboundLimits (array of NTTInboundLimit per chain pair)
Chain ID constants defined: CELO_EVM_CHAIN_ID=42220, CELO_WH_CHAIN_ID=14, MONAD_EVM_CHAIN_ID=143, MONAD_WH_CHAIN_ID=48
Rate limit constant: DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT = 100_000e18
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Token addresses are NOT hardcoded hex - they are addressbook labels (strings) resolved at runtime via lookupAddressbook(). Wormhole infra (core bridge) also resolved via addressbook. The config only stores the topology, modes, and limits.

userStories.title: Add USDm and GBPm token configurations to NTTConfig
userStories.description: As a developer, I want getUSDmConfig() and getGBPmConfig() functions in NTTConfig returning the complete bridge topology for each token.
acceptanceCriteria: NTTConfig.sol has getUSDmConfig() returning NTTTokenConfig for USDm
USDm config: tokenDecimals=18, ownerLabel='MigrationMultisig'
USDm Celo chain: tokenLabel='StableTokenV2USD', isBurning=true, outboundLimit=100_000e18
USDm Monad chain: tokenLabel='StableTokenSpokeUSD', isBurning=true, outboundLimit=100_000e18
USDm inbound limits: 100_000e18 in both directions (Celo<->Monad)
NTTConfig.sol has getGBPmConfig() returning NTTTokenConfig for GBPm
GBPm config: tokenDecimals=18, ownerLabel='MigrationMultisig'
GBPm Celo chain: tokenLabel='StableTokenV2GBP' or correct label, isBurning=false (locking mode), outboundLimit=100_000e18
GBPm Monad chain: tokenLabel='StableTokenSpokeGBP', isBurning=true, outboundLimit=100_000e18
GBPm inbound limits: 100_000e18 in both directions
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Verify the correct addressbook labels by checking .treb/addressbook.json. USDm is burn-mint on BOTH chains. GBPm is locking on Celo (hub) and burning on Monad (spoke). The function should return a fully populated struct that scripts can consume.

userStories.title: Create DeployNTT treb script for NttManager and WormholeTransceiver
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want a treb script that deploys NttManager and WormholeTransceiver ERC1967 proxies for a given token on the current chain using CREATE3.
acceptanceCriteria: script/deploy/wormhole/DeployNTT.s.sol exists
Inherits TrebScript and AddressbookHelper
Annotated with @custom:senders deployer
Reads NTT_TOKEN env var to select token config (USDm or GBPm)
Loads config from NTTConfig (getUSDmConfig or getGBPmConfig based on env var)
Finds current chain config by matching block.chainid against config entries
Resolves token address via lookupAddressbook() using config's tokenLabel
Resolves WormholeCoreBridge via lookupAddressbook()
Deploys NttManager implementation via deployer.create3('NttManager').setLabel(tokenName)
Deploys NttManager ERC1967 proxy via deployer.create3() with ERC1967Proxy(impl, initData)
Deploys WormholeTransceiver implementation via deployer.create3('WormholeTransceiver').setLabel(tokenName)
Deploys WormholeTransceiver ERC1967 proxy via deployer.create3()
Calls NttManager.initialize() on proxy with correct mode, token, and chain ID
Registers transceiver with NttManager via setTransceiver()
Sets NttManager threshold to 1
Uses standard NttManager variant (with rate limiting, 24h duration)
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Follow the submodule's DeployWormholeNtt.s.sol pattern for proxy deployment. The submodule uses new ERC1967Proxy(implementation, '') for the proxy, then calls initialize() separately. CREATE3 naming: 'NttManager:USDm' for the proxy, 'NttManagerImpl:USDm' for the implementation. Import NttManager from mento-stabletoken-ntt/src/NttManager/NttManager.sol and WormholeTransceiver from mento-stabletoken-ntt/src/Transceiver/WormholeTransceiver/WormholeTransceiver.sol.

userStories.title: Create ConfigureNTT treb script for peers, limits, permissions, and ownership
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want a treb script that configures peer registration, rate limits, burn/mint permissions, and ownership transfer after NTT contracts are deployed on all chains.
acceptanceCriteria: script/deploy/wormhole/ConfigureNTT.s.sol exists
Inherits TrebScript and AddressbookHelper
Annotated with @custom:senders deployer
Reads NTT_TOKEN env var to select token config
Resolves local NttManager and WormholeTransceiver via lookup() from treb registry
For each remote chain: resolves remote NttManager via cross-chain lookup(identifier, namespace, remoteChainId)
For each remote chain: resolves remote WormholeTransceiver via cross-chain lookup()
Calls NttManager.setPeer() with remote Wormhole chain ID, remote manager as bytes32, remote decimals, and inbound limit
Calls WormholeTransceiver.setWormholePeer() with remote Wormhole chain ID and remote transceiver as bytes32
Sets outbound rate limit on NttManager
If burning mode: grants burn/mint roles to NttManager on the token contract
Transfers NttManager ownership to addressbook owner (MigrationMultisig)
Transfers WormholeTransceiver ownership to addressbook owner
All operations are idempotent: checks current state before writing
EVM addresses correctly converted to bytes32 (left-padded with zeros)
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: setWormholePeer() is IRREVERSIBLE per the NTT protocol - script must verify correctness. Address conversion: bytes32(uint256(uint160(evmAddress))). Cross-chain lookup pattern: lookup('NttManager:USDm', namespace, '143') to get Monad address from Celo. Follow the idempotent pattern from the existing SetupNTTBridge.s.sol.

userStories.title: Create UpdateRateLimits governance action script
userStories.description: As a governance operator, I want an action script to update NTT rate limits without redeployment.
acceptanceCriteria: script/actions/wormhole/UpdateRateLimits.s.sol exists
Inherits TrebScript and AddressbookHelper
Uses governance sender (annotated with @custom:senders governor or multisig)
Reads NTT_TOKEN env var to select token config
Resolves NttManager via lookup() from treb registry
Reads updated limits from NTTConfig
Calls setOutboundLimit() if outbound limit differs from current on-chain value
Calls setInboundLimit() per remote chain if inbound limit differs
Idempotent: no-ops if limits already match
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: This is the first governance-sender script. Check how existing action scripts in script/actions/ use governance senders. The sender type depends on the treb profile (e.g. oz_governor for Celo, private_key for testnet).

userStories.title: Create AddSpoke action script for expanding to new chains
userStories.description: As a deployer, I want an action script to add a new spoke chain to an existing NTT bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: script/actions/wormhole/AddSpoke.s.sol exists
Uses deployer sender for deployment on the new spoke
Deploys NttManager + WormholeTransceiver on the new spoke chain (reuses DeployNTT logic)
Configures peers on the new spoke pointing to all existing chains
Sets rate limits for the new spoke
Grants burn/mint permissions if spoke is in burning mode
Documents that existing chains need a separate governance action to add the new spoke as peer
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: Adding a spoke is two-phase: (1) deploy + configure on new spoke (deployer), (2) add new spoke as peer on all existing chains (governance action on each chain). This script handles phase 1. Phase 2 can reuse ConfigureNTT or be a manual governance proposal.

userStories.title: Create UpgradeNttManager and UpgradeWormholeTransceiver action scripts
userStories.description: As a governance operator, I want action scripts to upgrade NttManager or WormholeTransceiver implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: script/actions/wormhole/UpgradeNttManager.s.sol exists
script/actions/wormhole/UpgradeWormholeTransceiver.s.sol exists
Both use governance sender
Both read NTT_TOKEN env var
Both resolve proxy addresses via lookup() from treb registry
UpgradeNttManager deploys new NttManager implementation via CREATE3 with a new label (version bump via env var or config)
UpgradeNttManager calls upgrade() on the proxy to point to new implementation
UpgradeWormholeTransceiver follows the same pattern for the transceiver
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: The NTT proxy upgrade pattern uses the Implementation.sol base contract which provides upgrade(). Check the submodule's UpgradeWormholeTransceiver.s.sol for the exact upgrade flow.

userStories.title: Create TransferOwnership action script
userStories.description: As a governance operator, I want an action script to transfer NTT contract ownership between multisigs or governance contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: script/actions/wormhole/TransferOwnership.s.sol exists
Uses governance sender (current owner)
Reads NTT_TOKEN env var and NEW_OWNER_LABEL env var (addressbook label for new owner)
Resolves new owner via lookupAddressbook()
Transfers ownership of NttManager to new owner
Transfers ownership of WormholeTransceiver to new owner
Resolves contract addresses via lookup() from registry
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: The NTT contracts use Ownable pattern (transferOwnership). Check whether NttManager and WormholeTransceiver also have a pauser role that needs to be transferred separately.

userStories.title: Create PauseNTT action script
userStories.description: As a governance operator, I want an action script to pause/unpause the NTT bridge in emergencies.
acceptanceCriteria: script/actions/wormhole/PauseNTT.s.sol exists
Uses governance sender (pauser role)
Reads NTT_TOKEN env var and PAUSE env var (true/false)
Resolves NttManager via lookup() from registry
Calls pause() when PAUSE=true, unpause() when PAUSE=false
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: NttManager inherits PausableOwnable. Check exact function signatures for pause/unpause. The pauser might be a separate role from owner.

userStories.title: Remove old JSON config approach and update documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the old JSON config files and parser removed so there's one canonical way to configure NTT bridges.
acceptanceCriteria: script/config/wormhole/USDm.json deleted
script/config/wormhole/GBPm.json deleted
script/config/wormhole/WormholeNTTConfig.sol (JSON parser library) deleted
script/deploy/wormhole/SetupNTTBridge.s.sol deleted
script/deploy/wormhole/README.md updated to document the new treb-native approach (DeployNTT, ConfigureNTT, action scripts, NTTConfig library)
foundry.toml fs_permissions for script/config/wormhole updated if JSON read permission no longer needed
No remaining imports or references to deleted files
forge build compiles without errors
userStories.notes: This is cleanup after the new system is in place. Verify no other files import WormholeNTTConfig or reference the JSON files before deleting.
